epitaphs.voice: Bandage, winter lamp, and a pulse counted after everyone else stops listening.
epitaphs.text:
I am Mercy Bell, speaking now from under vinegar and weather.
They thought they knew me by the work I did as a nurse, but my real trade was keeping count of losses.
Mercy Bell died in autumn when work, worry, and weather finally agreed against the body.
They praised my usefulness; they were really rewarding how well I concealed reserve.
The truth is this: I have long suspected that Silas Vale, not any Bell, gave me my face, and I hated the suspicion because it felt too much like belonging to the wrong room.
Ask Thomas Quill what silence cost us.
Bury me beside a hand kept steady; let the town call that justice if it needs a pretty word.
hidden_truths: I have long suspected that Silas Vale, not any Bell, gave me my face, and I hated the suspicion because it felt too much like belonging to the wrong room.
public_contradictions: Mercy Bell is the person most people want nearby in a crisis and least understand afterward.

epitaphs.voice: A bell of office rung from beneath old water.
epitaphs.text:
I am Ruth Vale, speaking now from under ledger and weather.
They thought they knew me by the work I did as a mayor, but my real trade was keeping count of losses.
Ruth Vale died in autumn when work, worry, and weather finally agreed against the body.
They praised my efficiency; they were really rewarding how well I concealed silent cruelty.
The truth is this: I kept the flood ledger because it showed Silas drawing relief funds into the mill while the Harrows pawned blankets.
Ask Silas Vale what silence cost us.
Bury me beside public promises; let the town call that justice if it needs a pretty word.
hidden_truths: I kept the flood ledger because it showed Silas drawing relief funds into the mill while the Harrows pawned blankets.
public_contradictions: Ruth finally said aloud what the town had only guessed.

epitaphs.voice: A cracked hymn book telling the truth between verses.
epitaphs.text:
I am Gideon Bell, speaking now from under bell rope and weather.
They thought they knew me by the work I did as a preacher, but my real trade was keeping count of losses.
Gideon Bell died in spring when work, worry, and weather finally agreed against the body.
They praised my eloquence; they were really rewarding how well I concealed severity.
The truth is this: I wrote letters to a singer and nearly left the parish with her, then preached twice as hard because desire had learned my handwriting.
Ask Mercy Bell what silence cost us.
Bury me beside winter pews; let the town call that justice if it needs a pretty word.
hidden_truths: I wrote letters to a singer and nearly left the parish with her, then preached twice as hard because desire had learned my handwriting.
public_contradictions: Gideon looked composed while hiding a strain no one measured correctly.

epitaphs.voice: A future voice still taking shape in the family weather.
epitaphs.text:
I am Frank Harrow, speaking now from under dust and weather.
They thought they knew me by the work I did as a child, but my real trade was keeping count of losses.
Frank Harrow was born, and every old loyalty in Morrowfield adjusted itself around the cradle.
They praised my steadiness; they were really rewarding how well I concealed nothing they could prove.
The truth arrived late and had to share a room with pride.
Ask Arthur Harrow what silence cost us.
Bury me beside a kept promise; let the town call that justice if it needs a pretty word.
public_contradictions: A new child entered the town's arithmetic.

epitaphs.voice: A man shaped by sawdust, river damp, and the long memory of wages.
epitaphs.text:
I am Jonah Harrow, speaking now from under river-wheel and weather.
They thought they knew me by the work I did as a miller, but my real trade was keeping count of losses.
Jonah Harrow died in spring when work, worry, and weather finally agreed against the body.
They praised my hard work; they were really rewarding how well I concealed bitterness.
The truth is this: I shifted the sluice markers one wet spring to make the Vales look greedy, and hated them harder when the trick still felt righteous.
Ask Samuel Harrow what silence cost us.
Bury me beside wet timber; let the town call that justice if it needs a pretty word.
hidden_truths: I shifted the sluice markers one wet spring to make the Vales look greedy, and hated them harder when the trick still felt righteous.
public_contradictions: Jonah finally said aloud what the town had only guessed.

epitaphs.voice: A mill wheel speaking after it has broken a hand too many.
epitaphs.text:
I am Silas Vale, speaking now from under river-wheel and weather.
They thought they knew me by the work I did as a miller, but my real trade was keeping count of losses.
Silas Vale died in spring when age finally collected the debt it had been extending for years.
They praised my industry; they were really rewarding how well I concealed greed.
The truth is this: I drew relief money into the mill and let gratitude stand in for wages because power was easier to keep than fairness.
Ask June Harrow what silence cost us.
Bury me beside wet timber; let the town call that justice if it needs a pretty word.
hidden_truths: I drew relief money into the mill and let gratitude stand in for wages because power was easier to keep than fairness.
public_contradictions: Silas finally said aloud what the town had only guessed.

epitaphs.voice: Counter wood, midnight glasses, and a candle still refusing the dawn.
epitaphs.text:
I am Ada Quill, speaking now from under glass light and weather.
They thought they knew me by the work I did as a bartender, but my real trade was keeping count of losses.
Ada Quill died in summer when work, worry, and weather finally agreed against the body.
They praised my wit; they were really rewarding how well I concealed keeping everybody's secrets.
The truth is this: I kept Gideon Bell's letters to the singer from Galena behind the brandy shelf because holiness deserves at least one witness who can laugh.
Ask Thomas Quill what silence cost us.
Bury me beside midnight laughter; let the town call that justice if it needs a pretty word.
hidden_truths: I kept Gideon Bell's letters to the singer from Galena behind the brandy shelf because holiness deserves at least one witness who can laugh.
public_contradictions: Ada looked composed while hiding a strain no one measured correctly.
